subject: Statistics
title: Repeated Measures Designs
description: Learn Repeated Measures Designs in one minute.

hook.text: Measure the same person before and after treatment. The change is the effect. Simple and powerful.

buildup.text: Repeated measures designs test the same subjects under multiple conditions or time points.

discovery.text: Each person serves as their own baseline. Individual differences cancel out, boosting statistical power.

twist.text: Order effects complicate things. Being tested once changes how you perform the second time. Practice improves scores.

climax.text: Counterbalancing — randomizing the order of conditions — mitigates but doesn't fully eliminate order effects.

punchline.text: Measure the same person twice. But beware the second time isn't the same.

quiz.question: What is the main risk of repeated measures designs?
options: Order effects — being tested once can change how subjects perform the second time
